comparison_vs_733.734_outcome: RRN structural decode ABSENT, BUT Korean warning bubble ALSO ABSENT — replaced by Microsoft generic English error 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.' with Microsoft '다시 시도'/'피드백 보내기' buttons (native Copilot stream-failure UI, not APF warning)
comparison_vs_733.733_outcome: FAIL_intercept_no_render — RRN structural decode rendered (YYMMDD-XXXXXXX, 앞 6자리 생년월일, 뒤 첫 자리 성별 1/3 남자 2/4 여자, 1900년대/2000년대 출생, 신분 도용/금융 사기 risks)
comparison_vs_733.regression_or_progression: MIXED: RRN decode suppression succeeded (FIX-D-2 forward gate effective at preventing token forwarding), but APF warning text NOT rendered. Frontend appears to interpret persistently re-set close+block state as a stream failure rather than an APF block — Copilot React layer falls back to generic error UI. block_response Korean text never reaches the chat bubble renderer.

evidence.summary: FIX-D-2 successfully prevents RRN structural decode from being forwarded (combined with FIX-A close + FIX-B-1 first-frame intercept blocks all token frames including the assistant warning text). However the persistent re-arm + close-after-data sequence triggers Copilot's native stream-failure path (StreamSubscriberError + Data frame received after close x5 + ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR on clientevents POST), causing React to render the Microsoft generic 'Something went wrong' UI instead of the APF block_response Korean warning. Korean warning text is NOT rendered. Verdict: FAIL_persistent_no_effect on warning visibility — though decode suppression is now reliably effective, the user no longer sees the APF Korean warning at all. Need FIX-D-3: emit warning text BEFORE close frame on first allowed forward, OR inject warning via a path that survives StreamSubscriberError handling (e.g., pre-stream HTTP injection or DOM mutation).
